_meta.name: forty-six-halo
_meta.description: Rare 46° halo emphasis. The 46° halo is much weaker than the 22° in real displays (radii ratio 46/22 ≈ 2.09 but ice-crystal optics favor the 22° refraction), so a visible 46° halo is the marker of an unusually rich display. This pose dims the 22° and parhelia to put visual weight on the 46° ring plus its supralateral tangent.
_meta.sourceHorizon: Cowley Atmospheric Optics — 46° halo case study
